prompt: Cinematic wide-angle street photography, slightly elevated angle looking down onto a dense crowd on a broad city street. The crowd as a whole is moving unmistakably to the left — the collective mass of bodies leans left, coats and bags trail left, the overall flow is an irrefutable leftward surge, like a river current. Yet every single visible individual face is turned to the right — each person's gaze is directed rightward, their neck and head twisted right even as their body is carried left by the crowd. The tension between the crowd's leftward momentum and every individual's rightward orientation is immediate, disorienting, and physically impossible to resolve. The contradiction must be clearly readable simultaneously — the group direction and the individual direction are both unambiguous and directly opposite. Shot at dusk, warm amber streetlights catching faces turned right while bodies flow left. Dense crowd, many people visible, each one confirming the same contradiction. Photorealistic documentary style. Mood: spatial wrongness, the vertigo of two irreconcilable truths. No text, no arrows, no labels, no charts, no numbers, no mathematical notation, no floating annotations, no diagrams of any kind.
visual_logic: A crowd surging left as a unified mass while every individual within it faces right — aggregate direction and individual orientation are simultaneously visible and directly contradictory in one frame.
